question: For the `Date` class (from Q12 or similar with `day`, `month`, `year`), overload the `<<` (stream insertion) operator to allow `Date` objects to be printed directly using `std::cout` in the format `DD/MM/YYYY`.

test_cases.input: Date d(1, 5, 2023); std::ostringstream oss; oss << d; std::cout << oss.str();
Date d(25, 12, 1999); std::ostringstream oss; oss << d; std::cout << oss.str();
Date d(9, 7, 2020); std::ostringstream oss; oss << d; std::cout << oss.str();

errors.message: The operator<< implementation does not pad single-digit days or months with a leading zero, violating the 'DD/MM/YYYY' format requirement.
